summary: Rare gem from the ’87 summer tour featuring a smoking All Along the Watchtower > Stella Blue stretch, but captured on an audience tape that is muddy and lacks sound‑board clarity.
review: Fans rave about this show as a killer performance from the 1987 summer run – especially Jerry’s soaring lead on All Along the Watchtower and the beautiful jam after Stella Blue. The recording, however, is an audience/matrix source with bass that sounds muddy and reverberant, falling short of true sound‑board fidelity.

ai_rating.rationale: The musical performance is praised highly (average show rating ≈ 4.7), while the audio quality receives mixed to negative feedback (≈ 2.8). Weighted combination yields a solid 4.1‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Bass is muddy and overly reverberant, lacking clarity; not a true soundboard recording. Captures raw crowd ambience but suffers from limited definition.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Second set is highlighted as a rare, high‑energy segment; overall show described as a killer performance from the ’87 summer tour despite missing some songs.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His intro lead on Watchtower is called sublime and he delivers a smoking hot solo later in the song.
band_member_comments.Phil: Reviewers note hearing Phil’s amp directly, indicating his bass was present but no specific praise or criticism beyond that.
